groups.title: Pre-built escalating sexting scripts are the sales unit
Tag every fan by relationship type and price accordingly
Casino psychology: keep fans in the dopamine loop and stay in control
Log personal fan details in notes so any chatter can continue the relationship seamlessly
Send 1–2 free teaser images before any PPV offer to prime the subscriber
One reusable script deployed across all subscribers = scalable chat ops
Personalized openers using username or profile detail to force engagement
Rapport, transition, then sell
Four-axis sub qualification (profession, location, age, hobbies) to size wallet and build rapport
Inject scripted rapport breaks and push-pull frames throughout chat to create chase dynamic
Never send mass PPVs — individual messaging generates 3-4x more revenue
Brief chatters on model persona: tone, emojis, typing quirks, niche
Train chatters with agency-owned PPV scripts rather than relying on their defaults
Set expectations via call + concise video/docs emphasizing common beginner mistakes first
Weekly async Loom chat reviews focused on new subscribers + high-ticket purchases
PPV Looping: Follow up within 5–10 seconds of any purchase to chain sales
PPV Anchors: Plant a story or fantasy 6–24 hours before sending the PPV to manufacture perceived relevance
Use passive teaser content (towel/shower clips) to make the fan initiate sexual escalation, not the chatter
Apply time-sensitive + VIP-exclusivity framing to every PPV offer
Segment subscriber list into 5+ tiers; send different mass messages per tier daily
Score every chatter on 5 KPIs to build a leaderboard before restructuring
Three-stage follow-up protocol for unopened/unpurchased PPVs — guilt-trip only as last resort
Use 'innocent yes-ladder' question to psychologically obligate the fan before sending a PPV
Organise OF vault into named script folders so chatters can locate PPV assets instantly
Agency founder must chatter personally before hiring chatters
Always wrap up a sexting session before logging off to prevent churn
Deploy a chatter supervisor on Day 1 as a proactive asset, not a reactive fix
Triage multi-account chat queues by subscriber spend history, not recency
Use narrative 'gambits' (e.g., broken phone, emergency) to collect money outside standard PPV sales
Offer content bundles to increase average order value over single-item PPV
Sell the intimate experience, not the content — fans pay for emotion, not pornography
When a subscriber objects to price, add more content first; only discount by removing content, not by lowering the price
Gate chatter hires on English, typing speed, and connection before training
Track which message templates drive the most sales and circulate winners across the team
Warm fans with a slightly discounted first PPV, then escalate price on every subsequent send
Tiered PPV price escalation: 50% steps for low spenders, 70% steps for high spenders; break $200 cap via tip + multi-unlock
Free-trial subs, monetize later via PPV — not on the sub price
Hard floor of 50/50 revenue split — never go lower
Never fold on PPV price — repackage content instead of discounting
Salary-model structure to maximize agency net-profit margin
Match deal structure to creator tier, not one-size 50/50
Sell content as an escalating PPV script ladder
Run a multi-platform traffic mix; no single source carries it
Build a perpetual stockpile of karma-farmed Reddit accounts via VA team
Use Reddit profile bio as the single OF conversion point; layer IG or Twitter as intermediate funnel where direct OF links are banned
Reddit organic NSFW traffic produces the highest fan LTV of any channel — $8–$35 on free pages
Use high-volume 7/10 static images on Reddit — never repurpose IG hook-driven reels
Scrape a 100k-subreddit master list keyed to creator physical attributes, then niche-match posts
Two-funnel X system: paid retweet drops plus comment-baiting
Threads 30-slave-account follow-bot: 1,200 targeted leads/day via viral-post follower targeting
Scale to 15–20 Threads accounts per creator; bottleneck is manpower and hardware
Use US SIM card + US-based uploader account to target US TikTok audience from abroad
Main + 2-4 Burner Account Structure to Grow X Presence Fast
Pay X Promo Pages $50–$150 per Post to Inject 500–2K Followers Fast
Snapchat + read-only Telegram channel as a ban-resilient backup funnel
Bootstrap Threads from existing IG account for follower carry-over
Build a Telegram-sourced S4S network before signing creators to pre-load launch traffic
7-day IG warm-up: algorithm training, niche engagement, delayed link
Differentiate every signal across repurposing accounts to prevent IG cluster-ban
Make repurposed Reels structurally different, not just color-adjusted
Every reel needs hook, retention, reward; 2-3/day across content pillars
10-day / 1-reel-per-day structured test before scaling posting frequency
Screenshot → GPT prompt → image gen → animation → CapCut caption: full AI reel pipeline
Strip AI metadata before posting or reach is throttled
Automate contract → onboarding form → calendar booking via Zapier
Run a structured onboarding call covering 6 key areas before going live
Build a model profile doc with chatting style, persona facts, and content inventory for chatters
Record a Loom, paste the auto-transcript into ChatGPT, and get a step-by-step SOP in minutes
Treat the vault as inventory: weekly script refresh and analytics pruning
Run 3-variant script tests with unlock-rate tracking before standardising any chatting SOP
Track all agency KPIs and A/B test every variable before committing to it
Per-source tracking links expose where revenue originates
Build backend capacity ahead of creator pipeline, not reactively after signing
Score every creator on gross profit, team hours, and operational drag
Run the business on a 4-part minimum viable operating system anchored by a weekly scorecard
Replicate local folder structure 1-to-1 on Google Drive as business-continuity backup
Use weekly invoicing explicitly as a trust signal, not just a payment mechanism
Offload non-core departments to third parties (accounting, traffic, chat) to buy back founder time before hiring in-house
Identify and solve one operational bottleneck at a time rather than fixing everything at once
Central account/team/finance trackers to remove owner from ops
Send a monthly NPS form to every active client and require a response to every score
Maintain a running 'wins database' — screenshots, metrics, clips — per client for retention and resigning
Hold weekly video calls with each creator to give account updates and collect feedback
Proactively disclose account issues to creators — hiding problems destroys trust faster than the problem itself
Set explicit month-1/2/3 milestones with creator at onboarding
Classify every creator A/B/C and allocate resources accordingly — cut C creators
Give high spenders 1:1 personalized treatment, never mass messages
Build parasocial fan connection via social persona BEFORE the OF subscribe to raise LTV
Assign a dedicated model-facing manager to celebrate creator milestones and prevent churn
Give every new hire a 'daily winning formula' — a repeating Notion task with linked SOPs for each duty
Commission-plus-bonus comp structure with daily ranked leaderboard drives consistent chatter output
Multi-stage application filter: attention check + AP score + template effort scoring
Discord live call with screen-share: WPM test + English test + physical setup camera reveal
Supervised shadow → monitored practice → independent KPI ramp for new chatters
Hire cheap offshore chatters but train them intensively and systemize the work
Promote staff up the five levels of accountability to remove yourself as bottleneck
Revenue-gated hiring ladder: VAs, then chat manager, then ops
Loom-video SOPs to eliminate training misinterpretation
Three core hires outside chatting: account manager, SMM, and model-facing manager
Daily feedback cadence to accelerate employee ramp 7x vs weekly
Every team member must own a single measurable number — vague roles produce vague performance
High revenue means nothing without margin — $100k revenue can yield $3k profit
Vertical vs. horizontal scale: choose model before building ops
Max out one creator fully before onboarding the next; avoid managing many small creators simultaneously
Do every role yourself first, document it, then hand it off
Founder should spend hours/day on the highest-leverage asset
Avoid low-ticket volume models: getting 2 premium clients is orders of magnitude easier than getting 50 low-ticket ones
Send 50 IG DMs/day; back off 5 below rate-limit trigger
Open with a conversion-problem hook; never pitch the agency in the first DM
Treat the signing call as a two-way interview with hard non-negotiables
Prioritize creator work ethic over follower count or physical appearance when signing
Target mid-tier creators (10K followers, ~$1-3K/mo) not top earners
Build personal brand (YouTube + IG) so top creators DM you first
Cold outreach is a volume-consistency-conversation game
Source creators across IG, Twitter, Reddit, TikTok, and prioritize referrals
DM prospects from a personal-looking account, not an agency page
Treat creator recruiting as a full internal department that never stops, not an ad-hoc activity
New agencies should master one creator before signing multiple
Identify shared traits of best creators and make acquisition deliberately pickier
Source first creator clients from personal network (exes, classmates, hometown contacts)
Build distinct creator brands (audience + identity + story) — not just niches
Reject any reel missing aesthetics + emotional curve + pattern-interrupt — reshoot it
Stop volume-posting; define creator persona/brand before scaling content
Use a five-category taxonomy to separate OF feed, PPV, SFW social, dating-app, and Reddit-specific content
Format-match by platform: long-caption storytelling on IG, slideshows on TikTok, text posts on Threads
Assign each creator 2–3 content verticals matched to her strengths, then iterate on viral data
Coach creators on 4 non-negotiable quality standards: lighting, audio, background, and variety
Batch shoot 5 looks → batch schedule 5 days of page posts + paired PPV messages
Two-content-type IG framework: 'filler' (lifestyle) keeps algorithm happy; 'vertical' (viral-engineered) drives new traffic
Scale organic social ops as the #1 agency growth lever in 2026
Keep the profile wall teasing, never explicit, to preserve PPV value
Never link OF/Fanvue directly; warm accounts before linking
IG mother/slave split: main account accumulates reach; disposable slave holds the OF link
Treat every revenue-generating asset as likely to disappear tomorrow; diversify across creators, accounts, and traffic sources
Pre-warm satellite IG accounts as instant replacement bench when main account is banned
An AI chatbot can replace much of the chat team at a fraction of the cost
Let AI sort and convert new subs; route spenders to human chatters
Real creator + AI clone, kept on OnlyFans
Real models beat AI on connection, customs and platform access
